title: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
summary: The repository artifact is a copy or version of University of Michigan electronic item AHA2740.0001.001. U-M selectively encoded Andrew Clark's two-part 1907-1913 EETS edition and linked it to a 322-image composite carrier. Clark's 1907 Part I transcribes two distinct historical components of TNA E 164/26: an unfoliated title and partial chapter-table fragment of Nicholas Love's Mirror, then the Middle English Oseney register at numbered ff. 1r-61r. The register translates the second Latin Oseney cartulary, Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), through approximately f. 93v. Clark's comparison depended materially on H. E. Salter's working transcript and communications; selected Cotton Vitellius E XV readings do not make that earlier first Latin register the English translation's exemplar. The host's later Latin papal-confirmation section at ff. 66r-112v is outside Clark's target. Complete Clark scans and a complete 322-canvas U-M image bundle are cached; current catalogs expose no public complete facsimile of any of the three physical manuscripts.
review_notes: The edition-level composite, two historical components, three physical manuscript witnesses, modern scholarly edition parts, U-M text encoding, U-M image carrier, Internet Archive scans, and repository XML remain separate entities.

TNA E 164/26 is a composite host: section A is only a title/partial contents-table fragment of Nicholas Love's Mirror; section B is the incomplete English register; section C is a later Latin papal-confirmation collection at ff. 66r-112v outside the target.

Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343) is the second Latin register and stated translation exemplar. Cotton MS Vitellius E XV is the earlier first Latin register and contributes only selected comparison readings through Salter's mediation.
Clark's edition groups 279 DIV3 documentary/editorial units under 33 title containers; those units are not automatically 279 separate physical charters or literary works.

Clark's older Bonaventure and John Morton shorthand is not promoted to preferred authorship. Modern evidence ties the fragment to Nicholas Love's translation/adaptation of the pseudo-Bonaventuran Meditationes vitae Christi, whose Latin authorship remains disputed.

Current U-M No Copyright-United States language, the conditional 2006 XML availability notice, and the repository's broad CC BY-SA output statement are preserved as separate, unresolved rights evidence.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2740
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The pinned submodule source is not modified by these manifests.

entities.label: University of Michigan AHA2740.0001.001 electronic text
entities.description: A Level 1 structured electronic representation assembled selectively from Clark's 1907 and 1913 issue parts, with 322 linked page-image references.
notes: The XML includes wrappers, 1913 title and contents matter, Mirror pp. 1-4, register pp. 5-209, two indexes, and omission milestones for untranscribed forewords, grammar, and back matter.
The header states 322 600-dpi TIFF G4 source images, uncorrected OCR, no content editing, and TEI Libraries Level 1 encoding.

entities.label: University of Michigan AHA2740.0001.001 page-image sequence
entities.description: A 322-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 image carrier combining surfaces from Clark's two issue parts. The sequence includes matter represented only by omission milestones in the XML and is therefore distinct from the structured text.
entities.reproduction_format: 322 grayscale page-image derivatives exposed through IIIF Image API endpoints; cached at width 1800 pixels in a complete ZIP bundle
notes: Canvas document URLs returned 404 during testing, while the corresponding /tile/ Image API endpoints resolved successfully.
The manifest lacks an explicit license field; current item-level U-M rights wording is recorded separately.

entities.label: Clark 1907-1913 EETS edition of The English Register of Oseney Abbey
entities.description: The aggregate two-part scholarly edition: Part I supplies the target text in 1907; Part II supplies forewords, grammar, and indexes in 1913.

bibliographic.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, 2 parts, Early English Text Society, Original Series 133 and 144 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.; Oxford University Press for EETS, 1907-1913).
bibliographic.title: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 133 and 144

notes: The two issue parts must remain distinct because the target text and historical-source statements occur in different carriers and U-M recombines selected portions of both.

entities.label: Clark 1907 Part I: Text
entities.description: EETS Original Series 133, printing the Mirror fragment at pp. [1]-4 and the incomplete English Oseney register at pp. 5-209, with notes and Latin comparison readings.

bibliographic.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, Part I: Text, Early English Text Society, Original Series 133 (London, 1907).
bibliographic.title: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 133

notes: Clark supplies Roman title-group numerals from the Latin register for reference and records selected Latin/Cotton comparison readings in brackets and notes.
The edition stops where E 164/26 section B stops, during entry 278.

entities.label: Clark 1913 Part II: Introduction, Grammar, and Indexes
entities.description: EETS Original Series 144, containing the source-history forewords, grammar, and indexes rather than a second printing of the medieval target text.

bibliographic.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, Part II: Introduction, Grammar, and Indexes, Early English Text Society, Original Series 144 (London, 1913).
bibliographic.title: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
bibliographic.volume: Part II: Introduction, Grammar, and Indexes
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 144
bibliographic.pages: Forewords and grammar; indexes at printed pp. [211]-234

notes: A complete cached scan has no direct medieval target-text representation; it is retained because its source analysis and indexes materially explain the encoding and edition lineage.

entities.label: The National Archives E 164/26
entities.description: A fifteenth-sixteenth-century composite codex held at Kew. Clark distinguishes three abruptly bounded sections: (A) an unfoliated title and partial chapter table of Nicholas Love's Mirror, copied about 1450; (B) the English Oseney register, copied or translated about 1460 and numbered ff. 1-61, with text ending on f. 61 and ff. 61v-65v blank; and (C) later Latin papal confirmations at ff. 66r-112v, after 1513, outside the edited target.

holding.notes: The Discovery record says the record is open, held at The National Archives, Kew, and not digitised. Its edition note explicitly limits Clark's edition to fols. 1-61.

physical_description.support: Composite paper-and-vellum quires: bundles of four paper sheets laid inside one vellum sheet; stamped leather binding on boards with clasp remains and a vellum pastedown
physical_description.extent: Composite host through at least f. 112v; Clark target limited to the prefixed fragment and ff. 1-61
notes: All three principal sections end abruptly.
The full-host boundary matters for reproduction requests: ordering only fols. 1-61 omits the Mirror fragment, while ordering the entire host includes the out-of-scope Latin section.

entities.label: TNA Discovery catalog record C2960649 for E 164/26
entities.description: The current official metadata record for the physical composite witness, cached through the Discovery API.

entities.label: The Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ
alternate_labels: The Mirrour of the Blessed Lyf of Jesu Christ
entities.description: The abstract Middle English translation and adaptation associated with Nicholas Love, represented in E 164/26 only by a title and partial prefixed chapter table.
notes: The existing repository-wide abstract identifier work:LoveMirrour is reused for cross-manifest consistency.
E 164/26 is not evidence for a full continuous-text Mirror witness.

entities.label: Meditationes vitae Christi
alternate_labels: Meditations on the Life of Christ
entities.description: The Latin devotional work translated and adapted by Nicholas Love. Its traditional Bonaventure attribution is not accepted here as settled modern authorship; Johannes de Caulibus and other possibilities remain debated.

entities.label: Middle English Register of Oseney Abbey
entities.description: The abstract incomplete Middle English translated register represented by E 164/26 section B and Clark pp. 5-209. It is one documentary collection with 279 editorial entry units, not a single authored charter and not automatically 279 separate works.

entities.label: Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343)
entities.description: The second Latin Oseney cartulary and Clark's stated exemplar for the Middle English translation. The cartulary occupies ff. 9r-343v; the English fragment follows its order through approximately f. 93v, with one or two English deeds absent from the surviving Latin text.

date_statements.label: principal production
date_statements.value: late thirteenth century, perhaps 1280-1284, with additions through the sixteenth century

holding.notes: Current Christ Church and MARCO designation supplied alongside Clark's historical short form.

notes: Produced at Oxford in several hands.
The aggregate manuscript contains Latin, Anglo-Norman, and Middle English, but the source register translated in E 164/26 is Latin.
The current catalog says William of Sutton, abbot 1267-1284, composed and ordered the cartulary; charters extend to 1280 and later blank spaces received additions through 1474.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Vitellius E XV
entities.description: The earlier first Latin Oseney register, incorporating chronicles. It is not the English translation's exemplar; Clark records selected readings supplied through H. E. Salter for comparison with the second Latin register and English text.

date_statements.label: principal production and additions
date_statements.value: last quarter of the twelfth or first quarter of the thirteenth century, with additions through the first half of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: The current record offers physical-item request access and exposes no digitized-content link.

notes: The manuscript was damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.
Its text is primarily Latin and includes an Oseney cartulary with chronicles.

entities.label: H. E. Salter's working transcript and collation of the Oseney Latin register
entities.description: A modern scholarly working intermediary used by Clark. Clark credits Salter's own transcript of the Latin register and says nearly every page and line benefited from it; Salter also communicated selected Cotton readings. Its present survival, extent, and location were not established in public sources.

date_statements.label: use in Clark's edition
date_statements.value: before 1907 and during preparation of the 1907-1913 edition
date_statements.notes: Clark establishes use by 1907 but does not supply a production year for the working transcript.

physical_description.support: Unknown modern working transcript or notes
physical_description.extent: At least the Latin register portion relevant to Clark's English text; exact extent unknown

notes: The provisional catalog_description type follows repository precedent for a modern working transcript intermediary and prevents treating Salter's material as a second medieval manuscript witness.
This entity preserves the documented material intermediary instead of asserting direct Clark autopsy of the Christ Church and Cotton manuscripts.
The token-level allocation of Christ Church and Cotton readings within Salter's working material remains unresolved.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Clark 1907 Part I
entities.description: Complete 238-page PDF scan of the PIMS/University of Toronto copy of EETS Original Series 133, including the target text at printed pp. [1]-209.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 238-page PDF derivative from a 400 ppi scan

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Clark 1913 Part II
entities.description: Complete 116-page PDF scan of EETS Original Series 144. It contains source forewords, grammar, indexes, and society matter but no direct medieval target-text representation.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 116-page PDF derivative from a 500 ppi scan

The ZIP contains manifest.json, inventory.json, and 322 JPEG images; archive integrity was verified with unzip -t.
Every image was visually inspected through seven contact sheets. The derivative profile requests width 1800 and preserves grayscale rather than archival TIFF source resolution.

local_copies.label: TNA Discovery API details for E 164/26

access.access_method: Checked the current Discovery record, which explicitly reports that the item is not digitised; no public complete manuscript image object was found.
Obtain or bring a valid reader's ticket and book a standard reading-room visit at Kew, then order E 164/26 under current document-ordering rules.

Ask first whether TNA holds an existing complete surrogate or can reproduce the complete E 164/26 host, including covers or boards, spine, clasp, pastedown(s), endleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, the unfoliated Mirror section, English register ff. 1-61, blank ff. 61v-65v, and later Latin section ff. 66r-112v. If complete-host coverage is infeasible, use a page check to order the broadest approved section/folio set and preserve every boundary explicitly.
access.format: Digital or paper copies subject to item condition, page check, provider capacity, and rights clearance
notes: Current public guidance lists a £9.92 page-check fee and approximately 29 working days as of 2026-07-16; fees and turnaround can change.
Clark's published fols. 1-61 boundary excludes the unfoliated Mirror fragment unless it is named separately in the request.
Section- or folio-level ordering is a fallback after the complete-host/existing-surrogate question, not the primary preservation goal.
A request route is not proof that a complete-host reproduction will be approved.

access.access_method: Read the MARCO manuscript catalog record.
Checked the MARCO record and Christ Church imaging guidance; no public complete digital facsimile was found.
Contact Christ Church Library and Archives in advance as an external researcher, identify D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), and arrange a special-collections appointment subject to approval and condition.
access.format: Physical manuscript consultation by advance appointment

access.access_method: Ask first whether an existing complete surrogate or complete-host reproduction can be supplied for D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, and every recto/verso of all i + 353 folios. If complete coverage is infeasible, request the broadest approved research-image set and map the English source relation to cartulary ff. 9r-about 93v within the full cartulary at ff. 9r-343v.
access.format: Existing images or approved research photography, if available
notes: Current guidance says Christ Church is not accepting new high-resolution photography requests; an enquiry may still establish whether existing images or research-use options are available.
Requestability is therefore narrower than a guaranteed new complete high-resolution reproduction service.
Exact-folio ordering is a fallback after the complete-host/existing-surrogate question, not the primary preservation goal.

access.access_method: Read the British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog record.

local_copies.label: Current British Library Cotton MS Vitellius E XV catalog JSON
notes: Catalog description only; no manuscript images or target text.

access.format: HTML catalog description and JSON record

access.access_method: Checked the current item record, which exposes no digitized-content link; no public complete facsimile was verified.
Hold or renew a British Library Reader Pass and request Cotton MS Vitellius E XV for consultation in the London manuscripts reading room, using the current request route where required.
access.contact: British Library Reference Services enquiry form

access.access_method: Ask first whether an existing complete surrogate or complete-host reproduction can be supplied for Cotton MS Vitellius E XV, including binding surfaces, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, and every recto/verso of all 275 folios. If complete coverage is infeasible, request the broadest approved image set; exact Salter/Clark comparison loci are unresolved, so exact-folio ordering is a later fallback after passage identification.
access.format: Digital images under British Library imaging-service terms
notes: Current guidance gives up to 30 working days for delivery and places copyright and permission responsibility on the requester.
Supplied images may not be reproduced without written permission under the cited service terms.
A complete-host enquiry does not imply that conservation, service capacity, or cost will permit approval.

access.access_method: No current catalog, holding institution, or public copy was established from the edition statements and official witness catalogs consulted.
access.format: Unknown modern working transcript or notes
notes: Future archival research should search Salter's papers and EETS editorial correspondence before declaring the transcript lost.

Read the source-work and attribution discussion embedded in the Geographies of Orthodoxy Love profile.
Read the incomplete Middle English register representation in U-M item AHA2740.0001.001, beginning after the independent Mirror fragment.
access.format: Structured HTML text or complete scanned edition PDF
notes: U-M sequences 73-277 and Clark printed pp. 5-209 / PDF pp. 27-231 represent the register; the surrounding carriers contain additional matter.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML transcription and markup
rights.basis: U-M currently labels the displayed item public domain in the United States, the checked-in serialization retains a conditional 2006 availability notice, and the repository says non-public-domain output is CC BY-SA 4.0. No source proves which statement governs the imported transcription and markup layer or how the repository's output language applies to it.
notes: Do not treat the medieval text's public-domain status as resolving rights in transcription, markup, or repository serialization.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item AHA2740.0001.001, including structured text
rights.basis: The current U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain in the United States and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: Whether this current statement supersedes the conditional availability wording embedded in the 2006 XML remains unresolved.

rights.component: U-M page-image derivatives and IIIF manifest for AHA2740.0001.001
rights.basis: The current U-M item-level statement applies No Copyright-United States to the item's materials, but the IIIF manifest itself supplies no license field and does not state whether every image derivative is independently covered.
notes: The image bundle is a research cache, not evidence of permission to redistribute the derivatives outside provider terms.

rights.component: Clark's aggregate 1907-1913 edition, transcription, editorial matter, and indexes
rights.basis: Both issue parts were published in 1907 and 1913, and U-M currently applies No Copyright-United States to the represented materials.

rights.component: Clark's 1907 text edition and apparatus
rights.basis: The issue part was published in 1907 and U-M currently applies No Copyright-United States to the represented materials.

rights.component: Clark's 1913 forewords, grammar, indexes, and other editorial matter
rights.basis: The issue part was published in 1913 and U-M currently applies No Copyright-United States to the represented materials.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object and custody/access layer
rights.basis: Copyright status is not used to characterize physical custody or reading-room access. The Discovery record's Public Record(s) legal status does not by itself establish copyright or reuse status for manuscript content or modern images.

rights.component: Copies or images ordered from The National Archives
rights.basis: TNA says record images are outside the Open Government Licence and that publication or reuse may require permission; the physical record's legal status does not settle image rights.

rights.component: Discovery API catalog metadata
rights.basis: TNA licenses Discovery API catalog metadata under the Open Government Licence version 3.0, subject to attribution and exclusions.

rights.component: Medieval abstract Middle English work
rights.basis: The work was composed by about 1410; this statement does not extend to modern editions, transcriptions, descriptions, or images.

rights.component: Medieval abstract Latin work
rights.basis: The medieval Latin work is public domain; this statement does not extend to modern editions, scholarship, descriptions, or images.

rights.component: Medieval abstract Middle English translated register
rights.basis: The translated register was produced in the fifteenth century; this statement does not extend to Clark's edition, modern transcriptions, descriptions, or images.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object and custody/access layer
rights.basis: Copyright status is not used to characterize physical custody or appointment-based access; no item-level public facsimile license was found.

rights.component: Existing images or research photography supplied by Christ Church
rights.basis: Current imaging guidance requires direct enquiry and supplies no item-level open license for D&C vi.a.1; permission and fees, if any, are provider-specific.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object and custody/access layer
rights.basis: Copyright status is not used to characterize physical custody or Reader Pass access; no public item-level facsimile was found.

rights.component: British Library HTML and JSON catalog description for record 040-001103095
rights.basis: The cached JSON record exposes no user-conditions value and no item-specific catalog-data license. It is retained as research evidence under applicable British Library site and catalog terms, without inferring an open reuse grant.

rights.component: Digital images supplied by British Library Digitisation Services
rights.basis: British Library imaging terms place copyright-clearance responsibility on the requester and prohibit reproduction of supplied images without written permission.

rights.component: Unpublished modern working transcript, collation, and notes
rights.basis: The material is a modern unpublished scholarly intermediary of unknown date, extent, survival, and holding; no rights statement or public copy was found.

rights.component: Internet Archive page images, PDF derivative, and item metadata for englishregister01clar
rights.basis: The underlying 1907 edition is public domain in the United States, but current Internet Archive metadata exposes no item-specific rights or license field for this scan derivative.

rights.component: Internet Archive page images, PDF derivative, and item metadata for englishregister02clar

editorial_practices.representation: Section A title/chapter-table fragment and section B English register printed; section C Latin papal confirmations excluded
editorial_practices.description: Clark's target is explicitly narrower than the physical host. Part I prints the unfoliated prefixed fragment and numbered ff. 1-61 only; later Latin ff. 66r-112v are outside the edition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Unfinished or absent English rubrics and Latin-register titulus sequence
editorial_practices.representation: Thirty-three editorial title containers, with Roman numerals supplied from the Latin register for reference
editorial_practices.description: Clark preserves manuscript order while imposing a usable title-group structure. The unnumbered Rowlesham group and jump from XXXII to XXXIV remain visible; the edition does not settle whether XXXIII marks lost content or a dropped numeral.

editorial_practices.source_feature: English register compared with the second Latin register and selected first-register readings through Salter's working materials
editorial_practices.representation: Bracketed supplies, notes, Latin readings, and comparison-based editorial decisions
editorial_practices.description: Clark credits Salter's Latin-register transcript as affecting almost every line and records selected Cotton readings communicated through him. The edition therefore is not a purely diplomatic transcription of E 164/26.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Selected matter drawn from Clark's 1907 Part I and 1913 Part II
editorial_practices.representation: Wrappers/title/contents, complete historical target, and indexes transcribed; much of the forewords, grammar, and back matter marked as omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text is complete for the Mirror/register historical target but selective relative to the two printed issue parts. Image completeness must not be mistaken for continuous OCR or text-encoding completeness.
notes: Sequence 68 is a blank transition before the Mirror; sequence 278 is blank printed [210]; Index I begins in sequence 279 before the PB for sequence 280.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed source imaged as 322 600-dpi TIFF G4 files
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR with no stated content editing, encoded to TEI Libraries Level 1 and linked to page images
editorial_practices.description: The header explicitly characterizes the text as uncorrected OCR and Level 1 encoding. Apparent readings should be checked against Clark's scan or manuscript evidence before textual inference.

editorial_practices.representation: One recombined 322-image sequence interleaving selected surfaces from both issue parts
editorial_practices.description: The image carrier begins with wrappers and Part II preliminary/grammatical matter, then supplies Part I target text, Part II indexes, and society matter. It is complete for the U-M carrier but not a pair of complete volumes in original carrier order.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aha2740.xml, pinned from Kyvero-Press/CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT/BODY/BACK; all DIV1-DIV3, PB, MILESTONE, HEAD, P, NOTE1, L, and LG elements
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the repository artifact and U-M encoding: identifiers, 2006 header, 322 linked image count, uncorrected OCR/Level 1 practice, Clark source parts, composite content boundary, 33 title groups, 279 entries, pagination, foliation, and selective omission structure.

evidence.citation: Complete XML, Clark-volume, and U-M page-sequence comparison for AHA2740.0001.001.
evidence.locator: All 322 page-image canvases; Clark printed pp. [1]-234; XML front/body/back boundaries and omission milestones
evidence.summary: Verifies the recombined two-volume carrier, sequence 68 blank transition, Mirror sequences 69-72, register 73-277, blank [210] at 278, Index I at 279-282, Index II at 283-302, ads from 303, and the distinction between complete image coverage and selective continuous text encoding.
notes: The checksum identifies the XML side of the comparison; complete page-image and volume-scan checksums are recorded separately.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, AHA2740.0001.001, The English Register of Oseney Abbey.
evidence.locator: item title, table of contents, source statement, availability, and No Copyright-United States statement
evidence.summary: Confirms current public structured-text access, item identity and title, source-edition association, and U-M's present public-domain-in-the-United-States rights statement.
notes: Browser rendering succeeded; unauthenticated command-line retrieval returned HTTP 403 during review.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan AHA2740.0001.001 IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and complete page-image bundle.
evidence.locator: manifest.json, inventory.json, and all 322 images/*.jpg members
evidence.summary: The lowercase manifest supplies the richer current metadata and structure for all 322 canvases. The complete cached width-1800 grayscale bundle preserves every canvas, while work-portion locators identify historical target sequences 69-277 inside that full carrier.
notes: The manifest has no license field. Canvas document IDs returned 404, while exact /tile/ Image API endpoints resolved.

evidence.citation: Page-by-page visual inspection of all 322 U-M AHA2740.0001.001 canvases through seven contact sheets.
evidence.locator: sequences 1-322, especially 68-73, 277-283, and 302-303
evidence.summary: Confirms complete grayscale sequence coverage and the exact transitions: seq. 68 blank before Mirror title seq. 69; Mirror ends 72; register begins 73 and ends 277; 278 is blank [210]; Index I begins 279 and continues through 282; Index II is 283-302; EETS advertisements begin 303.

evidence.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, Part I: Text, EETS Original Series 133 (London, 1907), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title and contents; Mirror printed pp. [1]-4 / PDF pp. 23-26; register printed pp. 5-209 / PDF pp. 27-231; combined historical target PDF pp. 23-231 of 238; notes and folio milestones
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the complete edited target text, Mirror fragment boundary, 33 register title groups, 279 entries numbered 1-278 plus 117*A, title-numeral supplies, Salter-assisted Latin comparison, and abrupt final deed.

evidence.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, Part II: Introduction, Grammar, and Indexes, EETS Original Series 144 (London, 1913), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: source forewords; discussion of three E 164/26 sections and two Latin registers; Salter acknowledgments; grammar; indexes pp. [211]-234
evidence.summary: Primary edition-layer evidence for the composite-host boundary, approximate dates, Nicholas Love/older attribution claims, Christ Church second-register exemplar, Cotton first-register comparison, Salter intermediary, blank ff. 61v-65v, excluded Latin ff. 66r-112v, and Index I/II contents.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for englishregister01clar.
evidence.locator: item metadata, source institution, scan data, and file inventory
evidence.summary: Identifies the Part I Internet Archive object, PIMS/University of Toronto source, 400 ppi scan, and complete derivative file inventory. No item-specific rights or license field is exposed.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for englishregister02clar.
evidence.locator: item metadata, scan data, and file inventory
evidence.summary: Identifies the Part II Internet Archive object, 500 ppi scan, and complete derivative file inventory. No item-specific rights or license field is exposed.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, E 164/26, Cartulary of Osney Abbey, Oxon, Discovery record C2960649 and API detail record.
evidence.locator: reference, title, date, description, closure, legal status, digitisation status, holding, and edition note
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark and record ID, fifteenth-sixteenth-century date, Kew holding, open status, Public Record(s) legal status, not-digitised flag, physical notes, and Clark edition boundary explicitly limited to fols. 1-61.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Book a reading room visit.
evidence.locator: reader-ticket, standard-booking, document-ordering, and visit guidance
evidence.summary: Provides the current registration and reading-room booking route for consulting open original records at Kew.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Record copying.
evidence.locator: page check, copying request, fee, turnaround, condition, and rights guidance
evidence.summary: Documents the current route for checking and ordering copies of undigitised records, including the £9.92 page-check fee and approximately 29-working-day estimate current on 2026-07-16.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Copyright and re-use of materials supplied by The National Archives.
evidence.locator: record images, copyright, publication, permission, and Open Government Licence exclusions
evidence.summary: Separates rights in record images and supplied materials from open catalog data and warns that publication or reuse may require permission; physical Public Record status is not a blanket image license.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, Discovery for developers: Application Programming Interface and metadata licensing.
evidence.locator: API terms and Open Government Licence version 3.0 grant
evidence.summary: Confirms that Discovery API catalog metadata is reusable under OGL v3 subject to attribution and stated exclusions.

evidence.citation: Christ Church, Oxford, D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), Cartulary of Oseney Abbey, MARCO record ark:/29072/c09593tt47vq.
evidence.locator: summary, shelfmark, date, contents, production, language, support, extent, hands, and additional-history notes
evidence.summary: Identifies the second Latin Oseney cartulary as a late-thirteenth-century Oxford parchment manuscript, perhaps 1280-1284, with cartulary ff. 9r-343v, several hands, and additions through the sixteenth century; this is Clark's stated source register for the English translation.
notes: Tested .json and format=json variants did not expose a direct JSON record; the ordinary official catalog page was inspected and intentionally not cached as a faux deliverable.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library and Archives, Special Collections.
evidence.locator: external researcher enquiries and advance appointment guidance
evidence.summary: Provides the current mediated route for external researchers to request special-collections consultation by advance contact.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library and Archives, Imaging Services and Photography.
evidence.locator: current photography availability, existing images, research photography, permissions, and enquiry route
evidence.summary: States that new high-resolution photography requests are not currently accepted and directs users to enquire about existing images or research photography; no item-level open license for MS 343 is supplied.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Vitellius E XV, record 040-001103095.
evidence.locator: title, scope and content, 1175-1449 date range, Latin language, parchment, 275 folios, fire damage, digitized-content field, and access conditions
evidence.summary: Identifies the earlier first Latin Oseney cartulary with chronicles, its dates and physical characteristics, 1731 fire damage, current physical-item request route, and empty digitized-content field. It does not identify this manuscript as the English translation exemplar.

evidence.citation: British Library current physical collection-item request form and Reference Services route, read with Cotton MS Vitellius E XV catalog access text.
evidence.locator: Reader Pass, post-21-March-2024 renewal, advance request, London manuscripts-room, and availability-enquiry requirements
evidence.summary: Provides the current registration and request route for physical manuscript consultation and confirms that all manuscripts and archives must be consulted in London.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and Imaging Services terms.
evidence.locator: ordering, delivery estimate, conservation, copyright responsibility, and reproduction permission
evidence.summary: Provides the current paid image-order route, estimates delivery within up to 30 working days, places rights clearance on the requester, and says supplied images cannot be reproduced without written permission.

evidence.citation: Geographies of Orthodoxy, Queen's University Belfast, corpus profile for Nicholas Love's Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ.
evidence.locator: work identity, Nicholas Love responsibility, Latin source, dating, and authorship discussion
evidence.summary: Identifies Nicholas Love as translator and adapter of the Middle English Mirror from the pseudo-Bonaventuran Meditationes vitae Christi and distinguishes the uncertain Latin authorship from older Bonaventure shorthand.

evidence.citation: Geographies of Orthodoxy, manuscript affiliations and corpus description for Nicholas Love's Mirror.
evidence.locator: prefixed contents tables, day grouping, manuscript forms, witness count, and c.1410 dating discussion
evidence.summary: Shows that Mirror witnesses normally prefix chapter tables and group chapters by days, matching the formal features of the E 164/26 fragment, while leaving its exact recension and affiliation open.

evidence.citation: General Corpus README.org, License section.
evidence.locator: lines 58-65
evidence.summary: States that repository code is GPL-3.0 and that all non-public-domain output is CC BY-SA 4.0. The statement does not precisely define whether imported U-M XML transcription and markup are repository output.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International legal text.
evidence.locator: complete license text
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact CC BY-SA 4.0 legal text referenced by README.org; it does not itself resolve whether a particular imported source component is within the repository's licensed output.

open_questions.question: Which textual recension and manuscript family of Nicholas Love's Mirror does the E 164/26 title and partial chapter table represent?
next_steps: Obtain images of the prefixed unfoliated section and collate every heading against current Mirror manuscript inventories.
Keep Clark's Bonaventure and John Morton shorthand as edition history, not preferred modern authorship.

open_questions.question: How do Clark's 279 documentary/editorial entry units and 33 title groups map to physical deed boundaries in E 164/26, and does the missing XXXIII represent lost content or only a dropped numeral?
next_steps: Ask first for an existing complete surrogate or complete-host images covering binding surfaces, pastedown(s), endleaves, blanks, every recto/verso, and all three sections through at least f. 112v.

If complete-host coverage is infeasible, inspect or order the broadest approved fallback covering the unfoliated prefixed section and English register ff. 1r-61r while retaining blank ff. 61v-65v and later Latin ff. 66r-112v as host-boundary context.
Do not promote the 279 DIV3 units to independent physical deeds or separately cataloged works without direct comparison.

open_questions.question: Will TNA identify an existing complete surrogate or approve complete-host reproduction of E 164/26, including binding surfaces, pastedown(s), endleaves, blanks, every recto/verso, and all three sections through at least f. 112v?
next_steps: Submit an enquiry quoting E 164/26 and ask first about an existing complete surrogate or complete-host capture of covers/boards, spine, clasp, pastedown(s), endleaves, blanks, every recto/verso, the unfoliated Mirror section, English register ff. 1-61, and Latin ff. 66r-112v.
Use a page check and section/folio ordering only as a fallback if complete-host coverage is infeasible, while preserving every boundary explicitly.
Ask separately about condition, completeness, delivery format, publication permission, and image reuse terms before relying on the order route.
notes: No request was submitted during this research; status remains unresolved rather than implying an open external ticket.

open_questions.question: Where, if anywhere, does H. E. Salter's working Latin-register transcript or collation survive, and which exact Clark readings derive from Christ Church versus Cotton?
next_steps: Search Salter papers, EETS editorial correspondence, and institutional archival catalogs.
If found, inventory the transcript's extent and collate Clark's notes token by token before asserting direct source allocation.

open_questions.question: Can Christ Church supply existing images or authorize research photography for D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), especially the Latin register through approximately f. 93v?
next_steps: Email Christ Church Library and Archives with the current shelfmark and ask first about an existing complete surrogate or complete-host coverage of all i + 353 folios and binding surfaces.
Ask whether existing images, on-site photography, fees, delivery formats, and reuse permissions are available while new high-resolution orders are suspended.
notes: No enquiry was submitted during this research.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the conditional availability notice embedded in the 2006 XML, and does it cover the current IIIF image derivatives?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for written clarification before redistributing the transcription, markup, or cached image bundle.
Keep the medieval works, Clark edition, Internet Archive scans, U-M encoding, U-M page images, and repository artifact as distinct rights components.
